item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Somehow quiet and thoughtful, you know what it's about without understanding, I usealy dislike anomalies like this but this one is done well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Actually, that's not really accurate. In most orchids a single stamen is fused with the style, but a number of orchid genera retain separate stamens. More importantly, though, it's actually one of the least fantastical bits of the whole dart-flower thing to have them be made out of stamens, because orchids do all sorts of weird things with stamenodes1.(they're kinky like that) When you see an orchid with a frilly bit, or a hood, or one or two differently-colored petals, that's usually a stamenode.

Footnotes
1. nonfunctional heavily modified stamens that resemble exotically shaped petals

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One quick note, orchids by definition don't have stamens, so you might want to change that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice, original, interesting and, despite some over-creative parts, the writing is outstanding; this SCP is quite good indeed. Upvoted. I've got just one question, what do you mean by low and high Palacian language, what is that language?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So…some guys in the past threw away some emotions and were catch by leaves every autumn?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm torn on this- the writing is very, very good, even the things the SCPs are saying, which is incredibly difficult to do well. On the other hand, I don't get parts of it. Is the Miguel in the Io5 log the same as/related to the other Miguels? (One would think yes, but to me it was written like that Miguel was being investigated for unrelated reasons.) What's with the strange plants and what the things are saying? I had some ideas, and it's definitely in the plausible range of "artistic lack of clairty", but I just didn't feel like I knew enough.

Also, I like that there is a picture, but that picture just made me think of leafblowers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My one complaint is the rhyming in the penultimate log. Unless someone in the linguistics department is feeling super-artistic, there's no reason the translation should rhyme. Other than that, the logs really pulled me into the atmosphere you created, without really explaining anything. Not sure if I'm miffed by that or not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
During seasonal autumn and until

Eh?

Well done otherwise. Sweet and sad and a bit disconcerting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sounds like the answer you want to give is PC (as opposed to, say, tablet, smartphone, 3DS…)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ummm… Dell? dont know if thats what you meant or google chrome is what you want i believe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It shows in the right for me, sorry. :( What platform are you browsing this from? Also, glad you liked it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this, but can you put the picture on the right somewhere so that its not shoving the entire article down, not that you have too, it's just a suggestion. Other then that I think you did pretty well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added. Thanks for your feedback, and sorry for not adding the footnote; was lost in the last version of the item. ^^U

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Searching for the word "kinesonastic" only gives one hit, this article. If it's spelled correctly, you should provide a definition for it in a footnote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to chat for reviewing this over and over and over. Also thanks to Photosynthetic for her help with the botanic aspects of the skip and, of course, for the Green Gale.

(Image is by me, posted in compliance with CC.)

Enjoy!
